questions.question: Which attribute on the tag provides alternative text for screen readers and when the image fails to load?
questions.explanation: The alt attribute provides alternative text that describes the image. It is essential for accessibility and is displayed when the image cannot be loaded.

questions.question: Which HTML5 element is used to provide a caption for an image?
questions.explanation: The <figcaption> element is used inside a <figure> element to provide a caption or description for the image or media content.

questions.question: What does the srcset attribute on an tag allow you to do?
choices: Specify different image sources for different screen sizes or resolutions
Set the source code of the image
Link to a CSS stylesheet for the image
questions.explanation: The srcset attribute lets you provide multiple image files so the browser can choose the most appropriate one based on screen size, resolution, or pixel density.

questions.question: Which HTML5 element provides the most control over serving different images for different viewport conditions?
questions.explanation: The <picture> element contains multiple <source> elements with media queries, giving full control over which image is displayed at different viewport sizes.

questions.question: The tag is a ___ element, meaning it does not have a closing tag.
questions.explanation: The tag is a void (or self-closing) element in HTML. It has no content and no closing tag, only attributes like src and alt.

questions.question: The ___ element is used as a semantic container for images, diagrams, or illustrations along with their captions.
questions.explanation: The <figure> element groups self-contained content like images or diagrams, typically paired with <figcaption> for a caption.
